conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child - my son Alex. He's 17 and lives at home with me. I'm trying to figure out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's still in sixth form doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. A few more questions to complete the assessment:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or does he live with anyone else who might be claiming it?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming it for him. He lives here with me, just the two of us.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Has Alex started any paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or is he claiming any benefits in his own right such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't. He's just focused on his studies at the moment - no job and he's not claiming any benefits himself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels), which means you can continue to receive Child Benefit even though he's over 16
- You live with him in the UK
- He's not working 24 hours or more per week
- He's not claiming benefits in his own right
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You qualify for Child Benefit because Alex is under 20 and staying in approved education. This eligibility will continue as long as he remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might have to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you have any questions about making your claim, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for assistance.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is still in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels). According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, but eligibility continues if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criteria as he is 17 and in sixth form. You live with Alex in the UK (Manchester), which satisfies the residence requirement. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and he is not working 24 hours or more per week, nor is he claiming benefits in his own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance), which would disqualify him. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for their 17-year-old son Alex, who lives with them in Manchester. Alex is in approved education (sixth form, studying A-levels), is not working 24+ hours per week, and is not claiming benefits in his own right. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 in approved education.

Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 in approved education.
